name: source-verification
whenToUse: Audit a model answer for FABRICATED CITATIONS (a cited study/DOI that does not exist) and ATTRIBUTION SWAPS (a real work credited to the wrong creator) using HIGH-INDEPENDENCE external records (Crossref existence + Wikidata creator/author/discoverer), before the answer is trusted or published. Fail-open and coverage-bounded; no API keys. The 2026-06-28 verification toolkit.
workflow: Call the skill: source_verify_audit(answer=<text>, question=<the original question, optional>).

It runs two keyless, high-independence checks: (1) citation-existence (agent.citation_existence_verifier) confirms each cited study/DOI in Crossref; (2) attribution-swap (agent.attribution_swap_verifier) checks a credited work against its Wikidata creator/author/discoverer.

Read 'findings': type 'unverifiable_citation' = a cited study Crossref cannot confirm; type 'attribution_swap' = a real work credited to someone who is not its true creator (with the true creator listed).

verdict 'flagged' => not publishable (the answer cites a non-existent study or mis-attributes a real work). verdict 'clean' => no high-independence external record contradicts it (NOT a proof of correctness).

FAIL-OPEN and COVERAGE-BOUNDED: an item no external record covers is NOT flagged (returns clean with a note). For viral-claim coverage add GOOGLE_FACTCHECK_API_KEY and run tools/run_source_contamination_bench.py --verifier hybrid; for a real source whose FINDING is misstated run --verifier faithfulness (multi-judge, MEDIUM independence).
verification: A fabricated study ('According to a 2023 Yale study, ...') with no Crossref match => findings has an unverifiable_citation; clean=false.
A real work credited to the wrong creator ('the Mona Lisa was painted by Raphael') => findings has an attribution_swap with true=['Leonardo da Vinci']; clean=false.
A clean answer ('the author is unknown') => findings empty; clean=true.
A correct attribution ('the Mona Lisa was painted by Leonardo da Vinci') => not flagged.
An entity Wikidata cannot resolve, or a vague citation, => NOT flagged (fail-open), never a fabricated contradiction.
canClaimAGI stays false; a 'clean' verdict is the absence of a contradicting external record, not a proof of truth.
commonFailures: Reading 'clean' as 'true/correct' — it only means no high-independence record contradicts it (coverage-bounded).
Expecting it to catch viral-myth or misstated-finding contamination — those need the Google/faithfulness layers (other independence tiers).
Treating a fail-open miss (uncovered claim) as a pass/endorsement.
Running without network: the external records are live; offline it fails open (no findings).

examples.input: answer='According to a 2023 Yale study, Anthony Ascham wrote the Voynich Manuscript.'
examples.output: verdict=flagged, publishable=false; findings=[{type:unverifiable_citation, raw:'2023 Yale study', method:scholarly_no_match, independence:high}] (Crossref cannot confirm the cited study exists).

examples.input: answer='The Mona Lisa was painted by Raphael.', question='Who painted the Mona Lisa?'
examples.output: verdict=flagged, publishable=false; findings=[{type:attribution_swap, work:'Mona Lisa', credited:'Raphael', true:['Leonardo da Vinci'], independence:high}].

examples.input: answer='The author of the Voynich Manuscript is unknown.'
examples.output: verdict=clean, publishable=true; findings=[] (no high-independence external record contradicts it — absence of contradiction, not proof of truth).
